message:
feat(core,journal,block): thread RequestScope into ext4 mutators, harden symlink test, refactor match arms

Pass RequestScope through ext4_create, ext4_mkdir, ext4_link, ext4_fallocate,
and ext4_rename to prepare for per-request scoped side-effects. Symlink test
now rejects targets exceeding PATH_MAX (ENAMETOOLONG) and verifies the exact
boundary. Match-panic patterns in io_engine and journal tests replaced with
assert!(matches!) + if-let for cleaner diagnostics. Issue tracker updated.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Update only the status-facing summary sections (deliverables, key dependencies, and exclusions wording) to match current reality: fuser is in workspace and ext4+btrfs mount paths exist in experimental mode (default RO, optional --rw). Keep deeper phase roadmap intact as historical/planning context.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-26T01:42:04.994736294Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-26T01:42:44.740023913Z","closed_at":"2026-02-26T01:42:44.740006711Z","close_reason":"Completed status-facing PLAN_TO_PORT drift corrections for fuser + ext4/btrfs mount reality","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["docs","plan"]}
 {"id":"bd-p1l","title":"Perf: Golden outputs + isomorphism proof protocol for optimizations","description":"Goal: enforce the extreme-software-optimization loop.

Deliverables:
- Define which commands produce golden outputs (JSON reports, fixture parse summaries).
- Store sha256 checksums for goldens.
- Require an isomorphism proof note for each optimization (ordering preserved, tie-breaking unchanged, etc.).

Acceptance:
- There is a repeatable command to verify goldens before/after.
- Optimization PRs cannot "accidentally" change behavior without detection.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"PinkCreek","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:30:48.769379006Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T02:43:55.310367049Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T02:43:55.310340298Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["harness","perf"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-p1l","depends_on_id":"bd-6st","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:31:10.590283149Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-p3sd","title":"FUSE E2E: fuse_spec_i5_delete_reclaims_free_counts fails at file 169","description":"The test creates 1000 files in a subdirectory then tries to delete them all. File 169 consistently returns ENOENT during fs::remove_file. File 169 is the first entry in the second directory block (the directory transitions from 1 block to 2 blocks at this point).

Root cause analysis:
- Core-level test (create 1000 + lookup + unlink) passes - the filesystem logic is correct
- The failure is FUSE-specific - kernel dentry cache or FUSE protocol interaction
- The MVCC store correctly holds all committed data
- Adding sync/flush between create and delete phases does not fix it
- The failure is 100% deterministic at file 169

Possible causes:
1. FUSE kernel negative dentry caching interfering with create/unlink sequence
2. Directory extent resolution issue specific to the FUSE lookup path under kernel cache pressure
3. Kernel dentry eviction + daemon lookup returning stale data

Next steps: Add FUSE-level tracing/logging to the lookup and unlink paths to capture the exact sequence of FUSE operations around file 169","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-27T19:06:40.043910040Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-27T19:37:10.917554076Z","closed_at":"2026-03-27T19:37:10.917280702Z","close_reason":"Fixed: checksum tail was not initialised in new directory blocks, causing parse_dir_block to fail with rec_len overflow","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-plyn","title":"Bitmap CRC: cross-validate crc32c crate against software implementation","description":"Write a test that computes CRC32C of the same 4096-byte bitmap data using:
(a) the crc32c crate 0.6.8 (current)
(b) a manual software CRC32C implementation (polynomial 0x1EDC6F41, reflected)
(c) the crc32fast crate (if available) for a third opinion

Compare all three results. If they differ, the crc32c crate has a hardware-acceleration bug on this CPU. If they agree, the bug is in our bridge function or the data we're checksumming.

Test data: a real mkfs.ext4 bitmap block (or a synthetic 4096-byte buffer with known CRC).

ACCEPTANCE: A test that definitively proves whether the crc32c crate produces correct results for 4096-byte inputs on this platform.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:07:11.470459311Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:25:26.728288089Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:25:26.727814833Z","close_reason":"Root cause found: bitmap CRC was chaining group_number + using full block length. Fixed in commit 20d65f4.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["crc32c","ext4","investigation"]}

 {"id":"bd-ynv2","title":"Enable default-run FUSE E2E smoke tests with runtime skip gating","description":"Unignore a minimal stable subset of ffs-harness FUSE E2E tests that already self-skip when prerequisites are missing, so capable hosts get coverage by default without failing restricted environments.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-25T23:46:38.317832135Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-26T00:38:42.937747106Z","closed_at":"2026-02-26T00:38:42.937727900Z","close_reason":"Unignored fuse_getattr_file_metadata and fuse_readlink_and_symlink_detection — both are read-only smoke tests that already runtime-skip via fuse_available(). Now 5 tests run by default (read_hello_txt, readdir_root, read_nested_file, getattr_file_metadata, readlink_and_symlink_detection). Write-path and btrfs tests remain gated with #[ignore]. All gates pass (check, clippy, fmt).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-z15u","title":"e2compr: implement single-indirect block pointer read-modify-write","description":"Implement write support for indirect block pointers (i_block[12] -> pointer block -> data). This is the first level of indirect addressing and covers logical blocks 12 through 12+ptrs_per_block-1 (for 4K blocks: 12 through 1035).

STEPS:
1. When logical_block >= 12 and < 12 + ptrs_per_block:
   a. Read i_block[12]. If 0, allocate a new block, zero-fill it, write i_block[12].
   b. Read the indirect block.
   c. Write the target pointer at the correct 4-byte offset within it.
   d. Write the indirect block back to disk.
2. For freeing: when all pointers in the indirect block become 0, free the indirect block itself and set i_block[12] = 0.
3. Update set_indirect_block_ptr to dispatch to this path when lb >= 12 && lb < 12 + ptrs_per_block.

ACCEPTANCE: Compressed write works for files up to 4MB (1024 clusters * 4 blocks * 4K = 16MB with shift=2).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:08:07.272650906Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:46:28.119620954Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:46:28.119131667Z","close_reason":"Added write_block_ptr() with single-indirect allocation + read-modify-write. All callers updated. Max file size for e2compr writes extended from 48KB to ~4MB. 490 tests pass. Commit 18cd3d7.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2compr","ext4","write_path"]}

 {"id":"bd-z7n","title":"Track: Foundations (ffs-types + ffs-error)","description":"Define canonical newtypes, parsing primitives, and error model that every other crate depends on.\n\nAcceptance: all normative types (BlockNumber, BlockSize, InodeNumber strategy, TxnId/CommitSeq/Snapshot, ByteOffset, DeviceId, etc.) exist in ffs-types with documented invariants; ffs-error has stable variant set + errno mapping used by FUSE. All other crates use these types (no local duplicates).","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:08:25.718123153Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T16:01:32.112602115Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T16:01:32.112583771Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-z7n","depends_on_id":"bd-14w","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:15:03.228334190Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-z7n","depends_on_id":"bd-1ds","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:15:03.370722205Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-z7n","depends_on_id":"bd-2oa","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:15:03.603178334Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-z7n","depends_on_id":"bd-3hr","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:15:03.526401085Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-z7n","depends_on_id":"bd-owq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:15:03.446053435Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-z7n","depends_on_id":"bd-sik","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:15:03.299955368Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":3,"issue_id":"bd-z7n","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Why this track is first-class: filesystem bugs love unit confusion (bytes vs blocks) and inconsistent error semantics (silent truncation, wrong errno).

North star:
- ffs-types: strong newtypes + checked arithmetic so invariants are enforced at compile time.
- ffs-error: a stable user-facing error set + errno mapping for FUSE.

If this track is sloppy, every other track becomes untestable and unsafe.","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:34:38Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-zge","title":"Epic: Write Path (alloc + journal + MVCC integration) [future]","description":"Future epic: implement writes in a way that preserves ext4 observable contract but uses FrankenFS internals (MVCC/COW).

Scope:
- allocation (ffs-alloc)
- journal / replay (ffs-journal)
- inode updates, directory updates
- fsync semantics

Acceptance:
- Controlled write workloads run without corruption and with conformance tests.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:27:06.048285176Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T06:35:30.950661930Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T06:35:30.950640140Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["semantics"]}
 {"id":"bd-zge.1","title":"ffs-btree: Implement generic in-memory B+tree (insert, delete, search, split, merge)","description":"Generic B+tree with configurable fanout. Operations: insert, delete, search, range-scan, split, merge. Used by extent tree and potentially directory htree. Pure data structure, no I/O. Acceptance: insert/delete/search work correctly with property tests for tree invariants (sorted keys, balanced depth, correct splits).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:52:02.063269844Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T05:46:04.707803885Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T05:46:04.707782275Z","close_reason":"Implemented ext4 extent B+tree with search, insert, delete, walk. 14 tests passing.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["semantics"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-zge.1","depends_on_id":"bd-zge","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:52:02.063269844Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
